introPhrase.voice1: Slight cackling can be heard in the distance
introPhrase.voice2: A small, green creature can be seen just ahead

introPhrase.voice1: You hear low growls
introPhrase.voice2: A canine bares its teeth

introPhrase.voice1: The stench of rot reaches you before anything else
introPhrase.voice2: A dead man walks, and he has noticed you

introPhrase.voice1: Black wings clatter overhead
introPhrase.voice2: A crow watches you with an eye too patient for a bird

introPhrase.voice1: Ribs show through its matted hide as it circles
introPhrase.voice2: Something starved has caught your scent

introPhrase.voice1: Tiny claws skitter across the dirt
introPhrase.voice2: A swollen rat drags itself toward you, leaking

introPhrase.voice1: A voice tells you to drop what you carry
introPhrase.voice2: A ragged man steps from cover, knife already drawn

introPhrase.voice1: Hooves slow to a walk behind you
introPhrase.voice2: He tips his hat, then levels a rusted pistol

introPhrase.voice1: A yip answers another, closer than the first
introPhrase.voice2: Yellow eyes catch the last of the light

introPhrase.voice1: A shadow wheels slow circles above
introPhrase.voice2: It has decided you are already meat

introPhrase.voice1: A dry rattle stops you mid-step
introPhrase.voice2: Coiled in the rocks, it waits for you to err

introPhrase.voice1: A spade scrapes against something it should not
introPhrase.voice2: He looks up from the open grave, unashamed

introPhrase.voice1: Chains drag in rhythm with muttered prayer
introPhrase.voice2: A hooded figure scourges himself, then turns to you

introPhrase.voice1: Scripture is spoken in a voice that does not believe it
introPhrase.voice2: He smiles and promises you salvation for a price

introPhrase.voice1: The cold deepens for no reason the sky can explain
introPhrase.voice2: Something tall and wrong unfolds from the tree line

introPhrase.voice1: Dry earth shifts where nothing should be buried
introPhrase.voice2: It remembers being wronged, and it remembers you

introPhrase.voice1: The air tastes of sulfur and old smoke
introPhrase.voice2: A larger cousin of the imp grins through the haze

introPhrase.voice1: The wind picks up grit and forgets to put it down
introPhrase.voice2: A shape made of dust and grievance takes form

introPhrase.voice1: A halo of dead flies hums around something holy and wrong
introPhrase.voice2: It was sainted once. Whatever answered the prayer was not God

introPhrase.voice1: A bell tolls though no church stands for miles
introPhrase.voice2: He has come for what is owed, and all debts are paid in full
